summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Red Team Framework v3.1 Attack Chains feature. All 19 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI fully functional with all 6 attack chains displayed correctly, phase selection working, and chain details with steps visible.
passed_tests: GET /api/ - Health check returns framework info v3.1.0
GET /api/chains - Returns all 6 attack chains with correct structure
GET /api/chains/web_to_shell - Returns full chain details with 4 steps
GET /api/chains/smb_to_domain - Returns SMB chain with 4 steps
GET /api/chains/kerberos_attack - Returns Kerberos chain with 5 steps
GET /api/chains/nonexistent - Returns 404 correctly
POST /api/chains/web_to_shell/generate - Generates commands with context variables
POST /api/chains/detect - Detects SMB chain from findings
POST /api/chains/detect - Detects Kerberos chain from findings
POST /api/chains/execute - Creates chain execution with execution_id
GET /api/chains/execution/{id} - Returns execution status
GET /api/chains/execution/nonexistent - Returns 404
GET /api/mitre/tactics - Returns 14 MITRE tactics
GET /api/tools - Returns tools list
GET /api/metasploit/modules - Returns MSF modules
GET /api/tactical/service-attacks - Returns service attack strategies
GET /api/tactical/waf-bypass/cloudflare - Returns cloudflare bypass strategy
GET /api/tactical/waf-bypass/unknown - Returns default strategy
GET /api/scan/history - Returns scan history array
Frontend - All 6 tabs visible (KILL CHAIN, ATTACK TREE, EXPLOIT, AI, OPS, CHAINS)
Frontend - CHAINS tab shows all 6 attack chains
Frontend - Chain details show steps with EXECUTE buttons
Frontend - Context inputs (LHOST, Domain, User, Password) visible
Frontend - GENERATE CHAIN COMMANDS button visible
Frontend - Kill Chain phase selection working (checkboxes toggle)
Frontend - Target input accepts text correctly
Frontend - ENGAGE button enabled when target and phases selected
Frontend - Status bar shows correctly
seed_data_creation: None required - Attack chains are predefined in AttackChainEngine class
context_for_next_testing_agent: Attack Chains feature fully tested and working. All 6 chains (web_to_shell, smb_to_domain, kerberos_attack, linux_privesc, windows_privesc, phishing_to_shell) are accessible via API and displayed in frontend. Chain execution creates execution_id and tracks status. Previous phase selection bug from iteration_4 has been fixed - checkboxes now toggle correctly.

attack_chains_verified.kerberos_attack: 5 steps - User Enum → AS-REP → Kerberoast → Golden Ticket
